text: Title: Who Turns Down Review Assignments? Authors: Jameson Beers Morrison Susan W. Hammond Journal: Political Science Journal Abstract: There are a growing number of new journals in the field of political science and many existing journals are receiving an increasing number of manuscripts, causing problems for editors and leading to the belief that reviewer fatigue is a major problem in scholarly refereeing. Our study shows that reviewer fatigue is only one of a number of factors that contribute to scholars' reluctance to review papers. Our findings suggest that scholars are willing to undertake review work, but that this entails interference with what are otherwise busy professional and personal lives. Keywords: reviewer fatigue, journal refereeing Abstract: Abstract: There are a growing number of new journals in the field of political science and many existing journals are receiving an increasing number of manuscripts, causing problems for editors and leading to the belief that reviewer fatigue is a major problem in scholarly refereeing. Our study shows that reviewer fatigue is only one of a number of factors that contribute to scholars' reluctance to review papers. Our findings suggest that scholars are willing to undertake review work, but that this entails interference with what are otherwise busy professional and personal lives.
explanation: Mostly plausible scholarly text, but the repeated 'Abstract:' and duplicated abstract indicate formatting artifacts or extraction errors that are more common in AI-generated or scraped data, creating a mixed signal.
